claims_audited.detail: References IRS Publication 17 and annual revenue procedures. Legitimate and verifiable.
References CFPB Regulation Z (Truth in Lending Act). Standard industry reference.
References FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Database). Legitimate source.
Generally Accepted Accounting Principles — standard reference. Not claiming GAAP certification.
States results are validated against Excel/Google Sheets. Reasonable verification method.
Changed to 'as quickly as possible after official changes' and 'when possible'. Removes hard SLA.
Methodology page uses 'industry-standard' and 'standard financial formulas' — no 'professional-grade' claim found here.

changes: Softened '48 hours' claim — changed to 'as quickly as possible after official changes'
Softened '48 hours of IRS announcements' — added 'when possible' qualifier
sections_verified: Formula Sources by category — YES (Mortgage, Investment, Tax, Retirement)
Update schedule — SOFTENED (was '48 hours', now 'as quickly as possible')
Data source examples — YES (IRS Pub 17, CFPB Reg Z, FRED)
User responsibility — YES (Limitations section: 'not financial advice, consult advisor')
